description: Realm incremental-marking state cleared at the end of GC instead of the start, leaving stale realm allocation state visible to the next collection. CVE-2022-45409 (sec-high, MFSA 2022-47, Firefox 107). GCRuntime stored per-realm flags such as wasAllocatedDuringGC to protect realms created during an incremental collection, but the vulnerable js/src/gc/GC.cpp code cleared that state only from finishCollection. If an incremental GC was aborted or ended early, the cleanup did not run; the next GC then swept zones using stale realm/zone state and could walk a dead zone or poisoned realm list during sweepZones, producing a debug zoneIsDead crash or ASAN null/poisoned-pointer fault. The fix moves the reset into the begin-mark/begin-collection path so every GC starts from clean realm incremental-marking state, including after abortgc or zero-budget slices.
